rationale: Both round-1 findings ADDRESSED in 82b28b2f. F3-2: tallyExitActionNonBead builds the recovery incrementally, appending --target only when target != ""; empirically, a malformed panel.json (unreadable registration) now yields `mindspec panel create demo --round <N+1> --spec <id>` with no dangling --target; readable-but-empty target routes to the transient Warn leg (no recovery command at all). F3-1: the function gained a gate param fed from reg.Panel.Gate (value type, safely "" when reg.Err != nil), and a live scenario with gate=final_review emits `--target 'spec/113-x' --gate 'final_review'`. Coherence intact: fix is CLI-layer only (zero internal/ diff, verified via commit stat), R1's non-bead exit path and R3's decision-inert Gate compose cleanly, escapeConfigValue+shellQuoteTarget disciplines are documented and non-contradictory (shell strips quotes on paste; gate values are enum-validated at create). go test ./cmd/mindspec ./internal/panel green.

findings.note: tallyExitActionNonBead now takes gate (from reg.Panel.Gate) and appends --gate '<quoted>' only when the panel stamped one; verified live: REJECT on a final_review-gated non-bead panel prints `--gate 'final_review'` in the recovery line. reg.Panel is a value type so the unreadable-registration edge passes gate=="" without a nil deref.

Recovery command built via strings.Builder; --target appended only when non-empty. Verified live: malformed panel.json yields a recovery with neither dangling --target nor --gate. New tests pin the quoting/escaping legs; the empty-target omission itself is scenario-verified but not directly test-pinned (acceptable — the Block path with empty target is only reachable via unreadable registration).

Empty-target Warn advisory renders a double space ("could not verify target  (transient...") since the %s target is empty in sanitizeNonBeadDecision leg 5b display text. Cosmetic, display-only, not a copyable command, predates 82b28b2f.

internal/panel/gate.go evaluates the transient-GitErr Warn leg (line 199) before the Rejects hard-block leg (line 245), so a non-bead panel whose target cannot be rev-parsed tallies PASS(advisory) even with a recorded REJECT. This ordering is the shared decision matrix internal/complete also uses, unchanged by this commit and pinned by earlier-round tests; noting for the record, not blocking.
